items.title: Customer contacts and addresses are soft-deleted and reinserted
items.risk: Sales orders could lose historical contact/address evidence if they reference mutable customer child rows.
items.decision: Sales order must store contact and address snapshots instead of depending on current customer child rows.
items.guard: beforeSalesOrder remains blocked until the snapshot contract defines required customer/contact/address snapshot fields.
items.futureAction: Draft and approve the sales-order snapshot contract before implementation.

items.title: PUT /business/customer is a full-update API
items.risk: Future partial customer changes could overwrite fields unintentionally if they reuse the full-update endpoint.
items.decision: Local partial changes must use dedicated PATCH or business-specific APIs instead of overloading full update.
items.guard: Current-context and sales-order review must call out the full-update boundary.
items.futureAction: Add a PATCH/business API proposal when partial customer updates become necessary.

items.title: Database direction is MySQL
items.risk: Switching design language back to PostgreSQL would create migration and SQL ambiguity.
items.decision: Use MySQL as the project database direction unless a separate approved migration plan changes it.
items.guard: Roadmap and context files must keep MySQL as the default database assumption.
items.futureAction: Review future SQL proposals for accidental PostgreSQL-specific assumptions.

items.title: CHANGE_SALESMAN owner-change mode is incomplete
items.risk: A backend or enum residue without a clear frontend entry can confuse owner-change workflows.
items.decision: Either add the intended frontend entry in a future customer change or remove the residual mode.
items.guard: Sales-order review must not depend on unclear owner-change semantics.
items.futureAction: Resolve in a dedicated customer/governance cleanup before order owner attribution depends on it.

items.title: business:customer:fund:adjust is reserved
items.risk: Ordinary roles could misuse a reserved fund-adjustment permission before a full flow exists.
items.decision: Do not expose or depend on fund adjustment until the complete adjustment flow is designed and gated.
items.guard: Fund-boundary contract must distinguish deposit entry, deduction, refund, adjustment, and reversal responsibilities.
items.futureAction: Define a dedicated fund-adjustment process before enabling the permission.
